title: [lora_l2.c:418] lichen_lora_l2_stop return value is always 0
description: Function: lichen_lora_l2_stop. Postcondition: The function signature returns int but always returns 0 (line 476). The documentation says 'Always returns 0 (stop is idempotent and cannot fail)'. This is fine for the contract, but the int return type suggests potential failure. Consider either returning void (breaking change) or returning the k_thread_join result to let callers know if abort was required. Currently the abort case is indicated by lichen_lora_l2_needs_reinit(). Priority 3 - API design note, not a bug.
close_reason: Fixed: lichen_lora_l2_stop now returns -ECANCELED when thread abort occurs, informing caller that module requires deinit/reinit cycle
